PHONE: 545-5821 
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY 
CITY HALL -
1400 HIGHLAND AVENUE -
MANHATTAN BEACH, CALIFORNIA -
9028E 
March 19, 1981 
Mr. Ted Prim, Executive Director 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street Mall 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Re: 
Request for Opinion Relative to Financial 
Interest Definition 
Dear Mr. Prim: 
This letter is written to request an opinion relative to an 
interpretation of Section 87103 of the Government Code and 
specifically subparagraph (c) of that section. 
The opinion 
request is made relative to the following facts: 
A member of the City Council of the City of Manhattan Beach 
is a Certified Public Accountant and is a shareholder in an 
accoun tancy corpora tion (CPA firm), hoI ding share s con sti tu ting 
approximately 11% of the ownership. 
The CPA firm will be pro-
viding accounting services for a corporation which operates a 
restaurant. 
Yearly fees to the CPA firm will approximate 
$4,000 but may be as much as $6,000. 
Historically, the profits to the CPA firm have been about 30% 
of gross receipts after expenses. 
Accordingly, the Council-
member's income from the client operating the restaurant will 
be approximately $4,000 x 30% x 11%, or $132 per year; assuming 
that the projected figures mentioned above are correct. 
An individual, who is designated Mr. Z, is a 10% limited partner 
in the restaurant corporation. 
Mr. Z is also a developer of 
certain property within the city which is proposed for a tennis 
court complex. 
The tennis court complex will be on property 
owned by the City and leased to a tennis club operation of 
which Mr. Z will be a principal. 


Mr. Ted. Prim, Executive Director 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Page 2 
March 19, 1981 
Does the fractional 10% interest by Mr. Z in the restaurant 
corporation which is provided CPA services by the Councilmember's 
CPA firm constitute a financial interest under the provisions of 
Section 87103(c), which would prevent the Councilmember from 
voting upon development matters related to the tennis club 
development? 
If I can provide any further information relative to this request, 
please don't hesitate to contact me. 
CKN :wu 
CARL K. NEWTON 
City Attorney

sections.question: Does the fractional 10% interest by Mr. Z in the restaurant corporation which is provided CPA services by the Councilmember's CPA firm constitute a financial interest under the provisions of Section 87103(c), which would prevent the Councilmember from voting upon development matters related to the tennis club development?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Council member who owns 11% of a CPA firm providing accounting services to a restaurant has a disqualifying financial interest under Government Code Section 87103(c) based on a limited partner's 10% interest in that restaurant when the partner seeks city approval for an unrelated development project.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present in this document. Document contains detailed factual scenario with complex ownership structure involving percentages and relationships. Some OCR issues visible in header but content is legible.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Manhattan Beach requests FPPC guidance on whether a Council member's indirect financial interest through a CPA firm creates a conflict of interest that would disqualify the member from voting on development matters involving a limited partner in the CPA firm's restaurant client.
